shell_pages.title: Overview
shell_pages.subtitle: Cross-shell route law, visual law, and continuity law for the Taxat frontend corpus.
shell_pages.hero_statement: This atlas is a contract demonstrator, not a speculative dashboard. Every page exists to keep later implementation agents inside the shell, selector, and recovery law.

shell_pages.title: CALM_SHELL
shell_pages.subtitle: Manifest and staff decision workspaces stay quiet, summary-first, and support-budgeted.
shell_pages.hero_statement: Four persistent surfaces, one dominant question, one promoted support region, and inline typed recovery.

shell_pages.title: CLIENT_PORTAL_SHELL
shell_pages.subtitle: Client-safe routes foreground the next task with plain language, strong continuity, and one support region maximum.
shell_pages.hero_statement: Five top-level destinations, contextual request detail, and no internal leakage across help, documents, approvals, or onboarding.

shell_pages.title: GOVERNANCE_DENSITY_SHELL
shell_pages.subtitle: Dense admin workspaces still preserve one dominant question, one promoted support surface, and route-stable filters, selection, and diff context.
shell_pages.hero_statement: Mutation is diff-first, receipts stay inline, and breakpoint compaction never throws away the selected governance object.

shell_pages.title: Continuity Lab
shell_pages.subtitle: Recovery, rebase, focus return, motion reduction, and hydration law across browser and native embodiments.
shell_pages.hero_statement: The lab turns continuity contracts into visible invariants and Playwright-verifiable behaviors.

shell_families.owning_object_rule: Manifest-scoped operator surfaces and staff collaboration workspaces stay inside the calm shell family.
shell_families.dominant_question_law: One dominant question and one dominant lawful action stay visible at all times.
shell_families.promoted_support_region_law: Only one promoted support region is mounted by default; compare and audit surfaces require explicit entry.
shell_families.native_embodiment_policy: Allowed in browser and native operator embodiments without creating a new shell family.

source_refs.rationale: Defines calm shell as a canonical family.
Freezes the four-surface calm-shell layout and interaction law.
Work item collaboration inherits the same four-surface shell.

shell_families.owning_object_rule: Customer request, upload, approval, onboarding, and help flows stay in the client-safe portal shell.
shell_families.dominant_question_law: Each client route foregrounds the next safe customer task in plain-language status terms.
shell_families.promoted_support_region_law: One promoted support region maximum outside the dedicated Help route.
shell_families.native_embodiment_policy: Browser-first shell with the same continuity contracts when opened through a native wrapper.

source_refs.rationale: Client request and approval flows are explicitly portal-owned.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, support budget, and constrained layouts
source_refs.rationale: Defines the portal support-budget and continuity rules.
Defines the portal selector profile.

shell_families.owning_object_rule: Governance objects, administrative mutation work, and policy inspection stay inside the governance density shell.
shell_families.dominant_question_law: Governance routes may show multiple structural regions, but one dominant question and worklist still lead the route.
shell_families.promoted_support_region_law: One promoted auxiliary surface by default; additional density requires explicit audit, compare, or contradiction states.
shell_families.native_embodiment_policy: No new shell family for native admin surfaces; future native work must remain a governance-density embodiment.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the governance topology and promoted-support law.
Defines the governance interaction layer and preserved context fields.
Maps the governance family to its design-token and behavior foundation.

route_records.dominant_question: What is the current authoritative decision posture for this manifest?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Expose only the next lawful manifest action in `ACTION_STRIP`; omit unsafe or non-material actions instead of disabling them.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Promote `DETAIL_DRAWER` only; compare, audit, and authority review open as explicit secondary states rather than permanent extra panes.
external_handoff_rules: Authority or identity browser handoff must return to the same manifest shell, object anchor, and focus anchor.
Detached comparison, audit, or packet windows remain support-only and must restore focus to the invoking parent anchor.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep `CONTEXT_BAR`, `DECISION_SUMMARY`, and `ACTION_STRIP` stacked in canonical order.
Collapse `DETAIL_DRAWER` inline or into a focus-preserving sheet before collapsing any primary summary or dominant action content.

source_refs.rationale: Manifest-scoped objects stay in the calm shell family.
Freezes the four-surface calm-shell composition.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, constrained layouts, and artifact handoff
source_refs.rationale: Defines same-object continuity and narrow-layout collapse.

route_records.notes: ASSUMPTION_MANIFEST_WORKSPACE_ROUTE_PATTERN: the browser route is normalized from the explicit manifest focus grammar and manifest ownership law.

route_records.dominant_question: What workflow issue attached to this manifest requires operator attention now?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Retain the manifest-level dominant action while opening workflow context as an explicit support-mode focus, not a shell switch.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Use the same `DETAIL_DRAWER` support budget while deep-linking the workflow module into focus.
external_handoff_rules: Workflow deep links restore the manifest shell and preserve the explicit workflow return path.
Notification-driven opens must highlight the focused workflow item without remounting a new shell family.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep the workflow focus in the same SPA shell.
On narrow layouts, retain the module focus and restoration target instead of discarding the active workflow context.

source_refs.rationale: Publishes the explicit manifest-to-workflow deep link.
Defines the deep-link fallback order for same-object restoration.
Governs parent return and list fallback when the original focus anchor is unavailable.

route_records.notes: Uses the manifest route as the owning shell while exposing workflow context as a route-stable focus jump.

route_records.dominant_question: Which workflow item requires intervention next?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Expose one authoritative row action per queue row after identity and triage signals; keep illegal actions omitted.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep queue detail subordinate; opening an item stays inside the same SPA shell rather than spawning a new route family.
external_handoff_rules: Queue-to-item transitions preserve filter chips, selection, scroll anchor, and return focus.
Notification-open restores the same queue slice or focused item row before escalating to list fallback.
responsive_collapse_rules: Preserve queue filters and selected row across breakpoint changes.
Queue -> item -> queue transitions stay inside the SPA shell at all breakpoints.

source_refs.rationale: Defines queue hierarchy, filter chips, and authoritative row actions.
Queue continuity requires preserving the lawful filter and return state.
Cross-device continuity includes notification-driven work item restoration.

route_records.notes: The calm shell owns the queue even when the work item body opens inside the same application shell.

route_records.dominant_question: What is the next lawful action for this workflow item?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Keep the workflow decision and next step explicit in `ACTION_STRIP`, with settlement-aware feedback in `CONTEXT_BAR`.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Use `DETAIL_DRAWER` for customer activity, internal activity, files, linked context, and audit without promoting contradictory panes.
external_handoff_rules: Authority and identity step-up flows must return to the same workflow item and module focus.
Closing explicit support windows restores focus to the invoking control or nearest lawful ancestor heading.
responsive_collapse_rules: Desktop may show a vertical module picker, tablet collapses to a segmented control, and mobile gives the active module full width.
Customer and internal activity threads never appear side by side on mobile.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the same four-surface shell and module taxonomy.
Defines keyboard order and breakpoint-specific module behavior.
Defines settlement-aware inline feedback and no-safe-action posture.

route_records.notes: This route is the canonical staff collaboration workspace inside the calm shell.

route_records.dominant_question: Which workflow module is in focus, and what action remains lawful from that focus?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Deep-linking a module must not rewrite the dominant action; it only changes the promoted support context.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Only the addressed module becomes the promoted support region, and it remains parent-bound to the workflow item shell.
external_handoff_rules: External handoffs must preserve the active module code and return focus anchor.
The workflow item remains the owning route even when module deep links are opened from notifications or manifest jumps.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep the active module code mounted on tablet and mobile even when the module picker compacts.
Never remap a module deep link into a different route family as a responsive workaround.

source_refs.rationale: Publishes the module deep-link grammar without leaving the SPA shell.
The route context and focus anchors remain part of the stability contract.
Module deep links still restore within the same governed object first.

route_records.notes: Module focus is route-stable state, not a permission to mount a separate collaboration shell.

route_records.dominant_question: What is the client's current required next step?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Exactly one dominant CTA stays inside `STATUS_HERO` with plain-language status copy.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep help, history, and supplemental detail subordinate beneath the primary task surface.
rebase_invalidation_reasons: latest portal object no longer matches cached route context
external_handoff_rules: Identity or authority browser handoff returns to the same portal route and focused request or approval.
Delegated acting-for identity persists across refresh, reconnect, and external handoff boundaries.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep the top-level tab selection, dominant CTA, and return focus when moving to narrow screens.
Stack support below the primary task rather than splitting the shell into alternate mobile-only routes.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the five-destination top-level portal navigation.
Defines the home route reading order.
Portal routes preserve semantic order before density.

route_records.notes: ASSUMPTION_PORTAL_HOME_CANONICAL_ROOT_ROUTE: the home route is normalized to the portal root path.

route_records.dominant_question: What document or upload action must the client complete next?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Upload affordances are frozen as browse, drag-drop, and camera capture; status phases remain explicit and typed.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep upload help and history below or behind the primary upload workspace.
external_handoff_rules: External authority or identity steps return to the same upload session and request focus.
Current and historical document artifacts remain separately addressable after recovery or reconnect.
responsive_collapse_rules: Resume the same upload session on reconnect in narrow layouts.
Keep file type and size-limit guidance visible without introducing a second promoted support panel.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the documents route reading order and upload affordances.
Requires reconnect-safe upload session continuation.
Route continuity keeps the portal interaction-layer contract intact.

route_records.notes: Status phases remain `[TRANSFER, SCAN, VALIDATION, ACCEPTANCE, REJECTION, RETRY]` in user-visible order.

route_records.dominant_question: What approval decision does the client need to make right now?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: The signature or sign-off action remains the only dominant action and must never target a stale pack.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Expose change digest and declaration detail as subordinate support around the sign-off posture.
external_handoff_rules: Signing or identity proof returns to the same approval and the same return path anchor.
If the pack becomes stale, the route must redirect to the latest pack before signing instead of letting the old sign-off continue.
responsive_collapse_rules: Retain the approval route and return path on mobile rather than remapping to a separate signing surface.
Keep change digest and declaration context available beneath the primary sign-off action.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the approvals route reading order.
Requires stale approval rerouting before signing.
Writable posture cannot conflict with the current legal approval state.

route_records.notes: Approval routes inherit the portal language contract and must stay client-safe under all recovery postures.

route_records.dominant_question: What onboarding step must the client complete next?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: The stepper workspace exposes only the next lawful onboarding action and omits inactive destinations.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep support content subordinate to the active step workspace unless the user enters Help.
external_handoff_rules: Identity proof or authority interactions return to the active onboarding step and its focus anchor.
Inactive onboarding navigation destinations remain omitted rather than disabled after recovery.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep the stepper visible before secondary help content when compacting.
Retain the same shell and return path across stacked narrow-screen layouts.

source_refs.rationale: Defines onboarding reading order.
Top-level destinations are omitted when inactive.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 3.1 One dominant question, one dominant action
source_refs.rationale: Each route retains a single dominant task.

route_records.notes: Onboarding remains a top-level route only while active and never becomes a permanent sixth portal destination.

route_records.dominant_question: Which help path or support handoff best resolves the client's current blocker?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: The dominant action routes into the most relevant help option while keeping case context visible and client-safe.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Help may devote the primary workspace to support choice and case context, but it still avoids contradictory competing panels.
external_handoff_rules: Returning from external help or authority flows restores the same case context and support option selection.
Help never leaks internal lifecycle, escalation, or audit-only objects into the customer shell.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep help option hierarchy ahead of deep supporting context on narrow screens.
Case context remains visible but subordinate to the help choice hierarchy.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the help route reading order.
Client help remains owned by the portal shell family.
Portal detail views must avoid internal audit leakage.

route_records.notes: Help is the only route where support content is allowed to dominate the shell without becoming a different shell family.

route_records.dominant_question: What request-specific action, artifact, or clarification should the client address next?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: The request detail route stays contextual: the parent tab remains active and the dominant action remains tied to the request focus, not to hidden internal state.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep exactly one promoted support region and preserve the explicit return path to the parent tab.
external_handoff_rules: Notification deep links restore request focus and explicit return-path control.
Identity and authority browser handoff returns to the same request route, focus anchor, and parent tab state.
responsive_collapse_rules: Narrow-screen recovery keeps the same parent tab, request focus, and return path.
No portal detail route is allowed to leak internal assignee, escalation, or audit posture as a compaction shortcut.

source_refs.rationale: Defines request detail as contextual, same-shell detail rather than a new top-level destination.
Requires notification deep-link restoration with preserved return path.
Back navigation preserves filters, selection, and focus.

route_records.notes: This route is contextual detail, not a sixth permanent top-level destination.

route_records.dominant_question: Which governance risk, configuration drift, or policy task requires attention first?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Promote one attention summary and worklist action at a time even when multiple structural regions are visible.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep a single promoted auxiliary surface by default while preserving inventory and audit context.
external_handoff_rules: Step-up or export actions must return to the same filtered worklist and selected object.
Governance overview receipts and problem notices stay inline and context-bound instead of launching separate status shells.
responsive_collapse_rules: At >=1440px the shell may show all structural regions; below that, the audit sidecar redocks before the workspace canvas changes semantic order.
Narrow layouts preserve selected object, dominant question, active filters, and promoted sidecar mode.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the overview layout and attention summary.
Defines one promoted support region by default in governance routes.
Defines breakpoint-specific redocking behavior.

route_records.notes: The overview route is the densest browser shell but still obeys one dominant question and one promoted auxiliary surface.

route_records.dominant_question: What tenant configuration change is being staged, and what blast radius does it carry?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Mutation is diff-first: configuration changes stage into a basket with explicit blast-radius and approval communication before submission.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Use inline policy help and blast-radius detail as a single promoted auxiliary cluster around the main configuration workspace.
rebase_invalidation_reasons: change basket invalidated by new policy basis
external_handoff_rules: Approval or step-up flows must restore the same staged diff, rationale text, and blast-radius context.
Receipts and failures stay tied to the configuration workspace rather than appearing as detached status views.
responsive_collapse_rules: Standard desktop and tablet keep the configuration workspace primary while collapsing auxiliary surfaces into drawers or tabs.
Narrow layouts preserve change basket, approval composer, rationale text, and filters.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the tenant route semantic reading order.
Requires blast-radius communication and approval requirements.
Mutation routes still keep one dominant action and avoid contradictory writable posture.

route_records.notes: Tenant mutation routes are governed by diff-first staging and never bypass the change basket or approval composer.

route_records.dominant_question: Which principal's effective access posture requires review or change?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Directory selection and effective access inspection drive the next action; simulation and authority chains remain support surfaces.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep one promoted auxiliary surface around the principal workspace even when the simulator is available.
external_handoff_rules: Step-up flows return to the same principal selection, filter slice, and simulator context.
Receipts must explain typed outcomes such as `ALLOW_MASKED` without dropping the current inspection context.
responsive_collapse_rules: Collapse auxiliary access panels before collapsing the primary principal directory.
Preserve roving selection and the selected principal across breakpoint changes.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the access and roles route family.
Governs the semantic order for governance access routes.
Requires explaining `ALLOW_MASKED` in the access simulation flow.

route_records.notes: Access routes share the same semantic order even as principals, roles, and simulator contexts vary.

route_records.dominant_question: What role policy or matrix change is under review?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Role matrix diffs and staged changes lead the route; the authority chain and simulator remain auxiliary.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Only one auxiliary surface is promoted while role diffs and rationale remain visible.
external_handoff_rules: Role-matrix diff and rationale must survive step-up and return intact.
Role edits return to the same matrix slice and preserve the authoritative diff basket.
responsive_collapse_rules: Preserve diff context and staged rationale through compaction.
Redock the auxiliary surface before collapsing the primary matrix workspace.

source_refs.rationale: Roles are part of the access route family.
Role matrix diffs must survive step-up.
Governance routes preserve filter chips and focus anchors as stability keys.

route_records.notes: ASSUMPTION_ROLE_DETAIL_WITHIN_ROLES_ROUTE_FAMILY: role detail overlays remain governed by the `/governance/access/roles` route family.

route_records.dominant_question: How would the current policy basis evaluate a governed access request?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: The simulation result and explanation lead the route; edits remain staged and contextual.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Use a single promoted support surface to show authority chains or matrix detail around the simulator.
external_handoff_rules: Return from step-up to the same simulator inputs and explanation panel.
Simulation outcomes remain typed and inline rather than redirecting to detached receipts.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep simulator inputs and explanation visible in semantic order when compacting.
Collapse authority-chain support before the simulator core.

source_refs.rationale: The simulator belongs to the access route family.
Requires typed `ALLOW_MASKED` explanation in the simulator flow.
The governance interaction layer defines the support-surface and selector policies.

route_records.notes: The simulator is a governance route, not a detached tool; it inherits the same selector and continuity contracts.

route_records.dominant_question: Which authority binding or handshake posture requires intervention?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Identity, binding health, and preflight readiness surface the next action; mismatch, delegation gap, and environment drift remain first-class status surfaces.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: One auxiliary surface at a time around the workspace canvas and audit sidecar.
rebase_invalidation_reasons: environment mismatch or delegation-gap state change
external_handoff_rules: External authority review flows return to the same link detail and preflight context.
Mismatch, delegation gap, and environment drift remain typed inline statuses after restoration.
responsive_collapse_rules: Collapse the audit sidecar before changing the identity and health timeline order.
Preserve the selected authority link and current preflight checklist state across breakpoint changes.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the authority links route and workspace module order.
Governance auxiliary surfaces remain support-only by default.
Requires mismatch, delegation gap, and environment drift to remain distinct surfaces.

route_records.notes: Authority links are a governance shell route even though they visualize external-authority posture.

route_records.dominant_question: Which retention policy requires review or change?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Policy selection and its blast radius lead the route; approvals remain explicit and staged.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Use one promoted auxiliary surface for policy impact, diff, or approval context.
external_handoff_rules: Approval or export flows return to the same policy slice, change basket, and rationale.
Receipts stay attached to the retention policy workspace rather than redirecting away from the selected policy.
responsive_collapse_rules: Redock auxiliary support before changing the semantic order of policy matrix and approval composer.
Keep selected policy, filters, and approval state through narrow-screen compaction.

source_refs.rationale: Enumerates the retention policy route family.
Shared governance topology governs support promotion.
Governance breakpoint rules preserve object selection and filters.

route_records.notes: ASSUMPTION_RETENTION_ROUTE_FOCUS_ORDER_NORMALIZATION applies to the retention policy landmark order.

route_records.dominant_question: Which legal hold requires action, review, or release?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Hold selection and typed hold posture lead the route; auxiliary evidence and approval remain staged support.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: One promoted auxiliary surface around the legal-hold register and current hold workspace.
rebase_invalidation_reasons: selected legal hold no longer resolves
external_handoff_rules: Approval or export flows return to the same legal-hold register entry and hold detail focus.
Inline notices must preserve the hold context and selected object anchor after recovery.
responsive_collapse_rules: Preserve the selected legal hold and current hold detail when redocking the auxiliary surface.
Collapse the sidecar before the legal-hold register or workspace body.

source_refs.rationale: Enumerates legal-hold routes within the retention family.
Defines `legal-hold-register` as a required semantic anchor.
Return behavior still preserves lawful selection and focus.

route_records.notes: ASSUMPTION_RETENTION_ROUTE_FOCUS_ORDER_NORMALIZATION applies to the legal-hold route as well.

route_records.dominant_question: Which erasure request or exception posture needs resolution?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: The selected erasure case and its approval posture lead the route; blast radius and supporting context remain explicit support.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: Keep exactly one promoted auxiliary surface around the erasure workspace.
external_handoff_rules: Approval and export flows return to the same erasure case, blast-radius context, and rationale anchor.
Problem notices remain typed and inline to the erasure workspace rather than remounting the route elsewhere.
responsive_collapse_rules: Keep the selected erasure case and approval state stable while redocking support.
Collapse auxiliary surfaces before the core erasure workspace.

source_refs.rationale: Enumerates the erasure route within the retention family.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 3.1 One dominant question, one dominant action
source_refs.rationale: Even dense governance routes preserve a single dominant task.
Governance routes preserve selected object and dominant question across breakpoints.

route_records.notes: ASSUMPTION_RETENTION_ROUTE_FOCUS_ORDER_NORMALIZATION applies to the erasure route focus order.

route_records.dominant_question: Which audit event, diff, or neighborhood comparison needs investigation?
route_records.dominant_action_policy: Audit tape selection drives the route; contextual diff and object neighborhood remain promoted support without becoming a second dominant workflow.
route_records.promoted_support_region_policy: One promoted auxiliary surface around the event diff inspector and sidecar.
rebase_invalidation_reasons: selected audit event no longer resolves
external_handoff_rules: Detached audit compare windows remain parent-bound support and restore focus to the invoking audit anchor.
Return from step-up or export preserves the selected event, comparison basis, and active audit slice.
responsive_collapse_rules: Redock the audit sidecar before changing the order of tape, workspace, and diff inspector.
Preserve selected event and comparison basis through breakpoint changes.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the audit route semantic reading order and module order.
Native compare and audit windows remain parent-bound support windows.
Audit return paths preserve focus and selection.

route_records.notes: Audit routes may launch explicit compare or review windows, but those windows remain overlays on the governance shell rather than separate shells.

source_refs.rationale: Maps calm-shell design tokens and behavior policies.
Freezes operator-specific interaction policies and notification surfaces.
Maps portal design tokens and behavior policies.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, support budget, and constrained layouts
source_refs.rationale: Defines the portal interaction layer and support-region law.
Maps governance design tokens and behavior policies.
Defines governance interaction-layer invariants and preserved context.

continuity_rules: The native primary workspace keeps the same dominant question, same object continuity, and the same settlement posture as the calm shell.
Mutation-capable affordances downgrade inline whenever recovery posture is non-`NONE`.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the primary native scene regions and calm-shell inheritance.
Native scenes are embodiments of canonical shell families.

continuity_rules: Secondary windows are support-only, parent-bound, and restore focus to the invoking anchor when closed.
Compare, audit, Quick Look, export, filing-packet, and authority-review windows never become a fourth shell family.

source_refs.rationale: Defines secondary native support windows and focus return.
Defines the semantic order for native support windows.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Full browser refresh or view reload without truth change.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: No truth change preserves shell, route, object, and focus invariants.
Refresh-compatible shells must publish continuity contracts.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Refresh may replay visibility-scoped updates, but it must not remount a different shell family or silently drop focus.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Live stream reconnect with monotonic catch-up or visibility re-entry.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Calm-shell reconnect must keep focus anchor and active detail module when lawful.
Reconnect and catch-up are governed by route key, object, access binding, and compaction state.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Reconnect remains inline unless the stream basis or access binding now demands rebase or access rebind.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Frame epoch drift, shell-stability drift, route-context drift, or compaction that invalidates the current surface basis.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.
invalidation_reasons: published frontier fell behind compaction floor

source_refs.rationale: Defines `REBASE_REQUIRED` when the frame epoch or shell basis drifts.
Compaction floor and publication frontier drive rebase decisions.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Rebase remains inline and explicit. The UI may downgrade mutation-capable actions, but it must not silently continue on stale authority.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Session, access binding, masking, or schema compatibility drift.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Defines `ACCESS_REBIND_REQUIRED` on session, access, masking, or schema drift.
Hydrated cache views must block mutation-capable actions until legality is restored.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Access rebind is fail-closed: cached content may remain readable, but mutation-capable or filing-capable actions stay blocked until the live legality basis is restored.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Direct deep-link entry from browser address bar, notification, or in-app jump.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.
invalidation_reasons: target module or request detail no longer resolves

source_refs.rationale: Defines deep-link fallback order.
Notification deep links restore request focus and return path.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Deep links first try to restore focus inside the same object. Only then may they fall back to summary or list anchors.

focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Queue continuity preserves filters and target highlighting.
Notification deep links must restore request focus and return path.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Alerts and notifications do not excuse shell swaps or filter loss. They just pre-focus an existing governed target.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Responsive collapse from desktop to tablet or mobile.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Semantic order is preserved before side-by-side density.
Governance compaction preserves filters, selection, and focus anchors.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Responsive compaction redocks support surfaces first and never introduces mobile-only alternate shell families.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Browser back, route return, or explicit back control.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.
invalidation_reasons: return target invalidated by truth change

source_refs.rationale: Back behavior preserves lawful filters, selection, drafts, and focus.
Queue -> item -> queue stays in the same SPA shell with explicit return routes.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Back navigation is a governed restoration path, not just URL history replay.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: macOS scene restoration or cached workspace hydration on app relaunch.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Native restoration uses scenes and `NSUserActivity`.
Mismatch requires purge and fail-closed mutation posture.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Native restoration reuses calm-shell law instead of inventing a route-less shell family with different semantics.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Closing a native or browser support window such as compare, audit, Quick Look, export, or packet review.
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Secondary-window return is a mandatory regression scenario.
Parent-bound support windows restore focus when closed.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Support windows never become primary shells; closing them must restore the invoking parent context.

focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Reduced-motion must preserve meaning with minimal or no displacement.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shared semantic selector and accessibility contract
source_refs.rationale: Reduced motion cannot change semantic order or action meaning.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Spatial motion becomes opacity, highlight, or state-color transitions without changing semantic order.

continuity_scenarios.trigger: Native or browser cache hydration on mismatched tenant, masking, or contract basis.
preserved_invariants: read-only legality until live basis returns
focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

source_refs.rationale: Purges are immediate on tenant, session, masking, or contract mismatch.
Illegal mutation must remain fail-closed during degraded or recovery posture.

continuity_scenarios.notes: Hydrated content may remain visible for continuity, but write-capable actions stay blocked until live legality is re-established.

visual_system.direction: Restrained dark, typography-led, quiet surfaces, one accent family, explicit status colors only.

source_refs.rationale: Premium low-noise product direction.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 3.1 One dominant question, one dominant action
source_refs.rationale: Quiet visual hierarchy serves the dominant question/action law.

typography.primary_stack: Inter, SF Pro Text, SF Pro Display, system-ui, sans-serif
typography.monospace_stack: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace
usage_rules: Use monospace only for ids, hashes, scope tokens, route keys, and schema or selector refs.
Keep headline hierarchy sparse so the shell law reads as a contract, not marketing copy.

source_refs.rationale: The product language is typographic and summary-first.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 10. Automation anchors and UI observability fencing
source_refs.rationale: Structural anchors must remain legible and stable.
The shell remains calm and structural rather than card-noisy.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shared design token and interaction layer foundation
source_refs.rationale: Density and spacing tokens remain shell-specific.

motion_contract.allowed_motion: Opacity changes and vertical translation up to 8px.
motion_contract.reduced_motion_policy: Replace displacement with opacity, highlight, or color-state changes while preserving semantic meaning.

source_refs.rationale: Motion stays low-amplitude and semantic.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shared semantic selector and accessibility contract
source_refs.rationale: Reduced motion preserves meaning and action order.

rules: Four-surface composition may show a vertical module picker and full detail drawer.
Primary calm hierarchy remains `CONTEXT_BAR -> DECISION_SUMMARY -> ACTION_STRIP -> DETAIL_DRAWER`.
Module picker may collapse to segmented control.
The drawer stays promoted but may redock into a shallower panel.
Active drawer module takes full width.
Customer and internal activity threads never sit side by side.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, constrained layouts, and artifact handoff
source_refs.rationale: Calm shell compacts by redocking the drawer before primary surfaces.
Collaboration module behavior changes at desktop, tablet, and mobile breakpoints.

rules: Keep one primary task column with subordinate help/history.
Portal tabs remain the only top-level navigation surface.
Stack support below the primary task before removing important task context.
Keep the same tab, same primary action, and same return path.
Resume upload, request, or approval sessions in the same route context.
No mobile-only alternate route family is allowed.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, support budget, and constrained layouts
source_refs.rationale: Portal support stacks below the primary task and keeps same-shell continuity.
Responsive fallback preserves semantic order before density.

rules: May show all five structural regions at once.
One promoted auxiliary surface still governs user attention.
Keep `WORKSPACE_CANVAS` primary and redock the audit sidecar into a drawer or tabbed inspector.
Preserve active filters, selected object, and focus anchor.
Move `SECTION_NAV` into compact tabs or menus.
Collapse `INVENTORY_RAIL` into a filter-and-selection tray and keep one promoted support region at a time.

source_refs.rationale: Defines the governance breakpoint rules.
Governance routes still respect one promoted support region by default.

assumptions.description: The corpus names manifest-scoped operator surfaces without a single literal browser root path, so the atlas normalizes the primary manifest route as `/manifests/{manifest_id}` to align with the explicit workflow deep-link form.
assumptions.impact: Manifest-focused route cards and the calm-shell overview use a stable browser pattern without inventing extra shell families.

source_refs.rationale: Manifest-scoped objects are explicitly owned by the calm shell family.
The collaboration contract explicitly publishes `/manifests/{manifest_id}?focus=workflow:{item_id}` as a route-stable focus jump.

assumptions.description: The client portal contract enumerates the Home destination but does not require a `/portal/home` literal, so the atlas binds Home to the canonical root route `/portal`.
assumptions.impact: Portal route tables and the atlas navigation keep a five-destination top-level model without creating a sixth path variant.

source_refs.rationale: Home is one of the five permanent top-level destinations.
The route architecture distinguishes contextual detail routes from the permanent top-level navigation.

assumptions.description: Retention policy, legal-hold, and erasure routes are enumerated explicitly, but their exact landmark order is less granular than the tenant/access/audit routes, so the atlas normalizes them through the shared governance reading-order law plus the named route-local workspaces.
assumptions.impact: The route landmark registry stays machine-usable while clearly marking the retention-family focus order as a governed normalization rather than a verbatim copy.

source_refs.rationale: Retention routes are enumerated at the route-family level.
Governance routes keep one promoted support region and a stable semantic order within the density shell.

assumptions.description: Native secondary windows are deliberate support overlays rather than a fourth shell family. They do not introduce route patterns, so the atlas models them as embodiment overlays on top of calm-shell continuity law.
assumptions.impact: Future native implementation work must preserve parent-bound focus return and must not let compare/audit windows drift into independent shell semantics.

source_refs.rationale: Native scenes are embodiments of existing shell families rather than a new family.
Detached native support windows restore focus to the parent anchor and remain support-only.

assumptions.description: The corpus names manifest-scoped operator surfaces without a single literal browser root path, so the atlas normalizes the primary manifest route as `/manifests/{manifest_id}` to align with the explicit workflow deep-link form.
assumptions.impact: Manifest-focused route cards and the calm-shell overview use a stable browser pattern without inventing extra shell families.

source_refs.rationale: Manifest-scoped objects are explicitly owned by the calm shell family.
The collaboration contract explicitly publishes `/manifests/{manifest_id}?focus=workflow:{item_id}` as a route-stable focus jump.

assumptions.description: The client portal contract enumerates the Home destination but does not require a `/portal/home` literal, so the atlas binds Home to the canonical root route `/portal`.
assumptions.impact: Portal route tables and the atlas navigation keep a five-destination top-level model without creating a sixth path variant.
